project.title: Metaproteomic of ARADs (Atacama Rover Astrobiology Drilling Studies) field campaign samples as part of the NASA ASTEP project carried out in September 2019.
project.projectDescription: Conditions of low organic matter content per gram of soil in the hyperarid core of the Atacama Desert, extreme temperatures and high UV radiation, makes it one of the best terrestrial analogue of Mars and one of the best scenarios for testing instrumentation devoted to in situ planetary exploration. We have remotely and automatically operated the SOLID-LDChip, an antibody microarray-based sensor instrument, as part of a rover payload during the 2019 NASA’s ASTEP ARADS Mars drilling simulating campaign. SOLID was loaded with a robotic arm with samples collected down to 80 cm depth and, after sample analysis with LDChip, sent results to a remote science team, to be validated by “omics” techniques.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Whole protein content from samples H1 (10-20 cm), H1 (20-50 cm), and H3 (40-80 cm) was extracted by the SDS-TCA method. Proteins were first concentrated into a single band in the stacking gel of an SDS-PAGE gel. After cutting the protein band, eluted, and digested with trypsin, proteins were ionized and analyzed on a Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer (Thermo) in DDA mode.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The acquired MS/MS data were analyzed using Proteome Discoverer software v.2.3 (Thermo Scientific) with search engine Sequest HT to identify the peptides against SwissProt Database. The acceptance criteria for proteins identification were an FDR < 1 % and at least one unique peptide identified with high confidence (CI>95%, p<0.05).  Protein spectral counts were normalized to spectral abundance factor (NSAF). Annotation to KEGG (Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes) and Clusters of Orthologs (COG) Databases was done on KAAS (KEGG Automatic Annotation Server) server, using the Single Best Hit method for amino acid sequence query) and on EggNOG-mapper v2 server. Taxonomy, protein names and sequences were obtained from the UniProt server using the UniProt Retrieve ID/ Mapping tool. Proteins identified in the negative control for contamination, as well as proteins belonging to Hominidae, were not considered for the analysis.

references.referenceLine: Moreno-Paz M, Dos Santos Severino RS, Sánchez-García L, Manchado JM, García-Villadangos M, Aguirre J, Fernández-Martínez MA, Carrizo D, Kobayashi L, Dave A, Warren-Rhodes K, Davila A, Stoker CR, Glass B, Parro V. Life Detection and Microbial Biomarker Profiling with Signs of Life Detector-Life Detector Chip During a Mars Drilling Simulation Campaign in the Hyperarid Core of the Atacama Desert. Astrobiology. 2023 23(12):1259-1283
